disclaimer: Years shown represent when each building turns 50 years old, based on New Orleans Property Assessor data, to be confirmed. This information is for reference regarding building age only. It does not imply that any building owner intends to make changes to their building at this time.
source: Orleans Parish Assessor's Office, data upload June 2026

anchors.name: Joe W. Brown Memorial Park
anchors.summary: The district's green heart — one of the largest parks in New Orleans, sitting directly on the corridor.
anchors.significance: Anchors the eastern-central stretch of the corridor with recreation, water, and gathering space — the "Nature" and "Water" in the district's identity.

anchors.name: Louisiana Nature Center
anchors.summary: An environmental education center inside the park footprint — rebuilt after Katrina and central to the district's nature story.
anchors.significance: Connects the district to environmental education and the lakefront ecology of New Orleans East.

visualizations.disclaimer: These images do not imply any commitment or decision by the building owners, nor do they reflect what the State would approve for tax-credit qualification. They illustrate stages of progress observed in other areas that have used programs like the Cultural District to raise awareness of the need to preserve and rehabilitate New Orleans culture.
